basics.name: Wookyoung Kim
basics.label: Senior Researcher
basics.summary: Thermal-fluid engineer specializing in data center cooling, heat exchangers, heat pump systems, and two-phase heat transfer. Experienced in both experimental research and engineering software development.

work.name: Korea Institute of Machinery and Materials (KIMM)
work.summary: Heat Pump Research Center, Department of Thermal Energy Solutions, Innovative Energy Machinery Research Division
highlights: PI: Jet-enhanced immersion cooling for next-gen data center cooling
PI: Thermophysical property iOS/Android app development
Lead: PCHE design & testing for liquid hydrogen vaporizers (down to -220°C, 100 MPa)
Lead: Immersion cooling waste heat utilization and thermal management
14 journal papers, 9 domestic patents, 3 international patents, 7 registered software programs

education.institution: KAIST (Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology)
courses: Dissertation: Study on a Thermal Network-based Model for Predicting the Thermal Resistance of Pulsating Heat Pipes
Thesis: Effect of artificial cavities on the thermal performance of pulsating heat pipes

projects.name: Jet-Enhanced Immersion Cooling for Data Centers
projects.summary: Development of high-efficiency jet-impingement immersion cooling technology for next-generation high-heat-density data center servers

projects.name: PCHE for Liquid Hydrogen Vaporizers
projects.summary: Design and testing of Printed Circuit Heat Exchangers for cryogenic liquid hydrogen applications below -200°C at 100 MPa

projects.name: Thermophysical Property App
projects.summary: Cross-platform iOS/Android app for thermal-fluid property calculations using CoolProp/REFPROP via WASM
